Question: Find the Taylor polynomials $$p_{0} , p_{1} , p_{2}$$ and $$p_{3}$$ for $$f ( x ) = \frac{1}{x^{2}}$$ at $$x = 1 .$$

Find formulas for the Maclaurin polynomials $$p_{0} , p_{1} , p_{2}$$ and $$p_{3}$$ for $$f ( x ) = \frac{1}{1 + x} .$$ Find a formula for the n th Maclaurin polynomial. Write your answer using sigma notation.
Find the first and second Taylor polynomials for $$f ( x ) = \sqrt{x}$$ at $$x = 4 .$$ Use these polynomials to estimate $$\sqrt{6} .$$ Use Taylor’s theorem to bound the error.
Use the fourth Maclaurin polynomial for $$\text{cos} x$$ to approximate $$\text{cos} ( \frac{\pi}{12} ) .$$
Find the Taylor series for $$f ( x ) = \frac{1}{2 x}$$ at $$x = 2$$ and determine its interval of convergence.
Answer: $$\sum_{n = 0}^{\infty} ( \frac{2 - x}{2^{n + 2}} )^{n} .$$ The interval of convergence is $$( 0 , 4 ) .$$

Question: Find the Maclaurin series for $$f ( x ) = \text{cos} x .$$ Use the ratio test to show that the interval of convergence is $$( \text{unicode[:-]} \infty , \infty ) .$$ Show that the Maclaurin series converges to $$\text{cos} x$$ for all real numbers x .
Answer: $$\sum_{n = 0}^{\infty} \frac{( -1 )^{n} x^{2 n}}{( 2 n ) \text{!}}$$ By the ratio test, the interval of convergence is $$( \text{unicode[:-]} \infty , \infty ) .$$ Since $$| R_{n} ( x ) | \le \frac{| x |^{n + 1}}{( n + 1 ) \text{!}} ,$$ the series converges to $$\text{cos} x$$ for all real x .
